summary: Iteration 2 full test of APDCL Connect comments/participants/admin analytics feature set. Backend: 13/14 tests pass (1 skipped due to author cascade side-effects from other test creating a query owned by AP10234 — non-critical). Frontend: admin lands on /admin, all 4 stat cards, monthly chart, top-queries, recent-comments, departments panels visible; admin sidebar shows 5 items with Dashboard highlighted and staying on /admin. Employee sidebar shows 4 items (no admin items). Both admin All Queries and employee Company Query show 'N of TOTAL employees reacted', WHO REACTED & COMMENTED participants list with Agreed/Disagreed badges, designation·department, and comment text with time-ago. Posting a comment updates the card in-place immediately. Backend POST/DELETE /api/queries/{qid}/comments works with proper 401/403/422 handling. Admin analytics endpoint returns monthly[6], this_month, top_queries (<=5), recent_comments (<=10), departments, and totals — 403 for employees. Cascade delete of query removes comments (verified no orphaned comment in analytics.recent_comments).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py enrich_query() sort key uses `-max(...).__hash__()` on ISO date strings which is non-deterministic and does not actually sort by recency — participants ordering may be arbitrary within the 'commented' group. Consider using the actual created_at string for lexicographic sort.
server.py is 780+ lines — starting to warrant splitting into routers (auth, queries, comments, admin, notifications).
admin_analytics performs N+1 queries in top_queries loop (per-query votes/comments count + author lookup). Fine for small data, will need aggregation pipeline at scale.
CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' (env fallback) — safe for preview but should be restricted in production.
test_credentials: Admin AP10000/Admin@123, Employees AP10234/AP10789/AP10812 with Employee@123 (AP10456 status unknown)
seed_data_creation: Test created transient comments prefixed 'TEST_' and a transient query 'TEST_cascade query' which was subsequently deleted. A few 'TEST_' comments may remain on seed queries (harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All feature requirements from iteration 2 verified working end-to-end. Login test-ids are login-employee-id-input / login-password-input / login-submit-btn / sidebar-logout-btn (not the shorter forms listed in problem statement). QueryParticipants component works on both /queries (employee) and /admin/queries (admin). Reaction summary format is exactly 'N of M employees reacted'. AP10234's query 'Hello Everyone' was created during this iteration and stays in DB.
